dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a Sick Person at the intersection of 1ST AVE and MEADOW BRIDGE RD.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
My name is Mark, I'm the one who called 911 for my friend.
What was the concern, Mark?
He was standing in the middle of the road directing traffic and jumping up and down. He looked confused and kept twitching.
How was he responding when you tried talking to him?
Can you open your eyes, sir?
Hmm….
Can you tell me your name, please?
Don't remember.
Can you squeeze my finger for me?
squeezes slightly
The patient is A&O*1 - inconsistently responds to verbal stimuli, as noted in the field.
Mr. Smith, we checked your airway and found no visible blockage or signs of obstruction.
Are you able to breathe normally?
-mm-mm…
Your breaths are 19 per minute, equal bilaterally, and your breath sounds are clear and equal.
Your hands are warm and dry - can you tell us when you last ate or drank?
Pulse is 114 - fast and strong.
Breath count is 19 per minute, regular, and shallow.
Blood pressure is 160 over 93.
Pulse ox is at 97 percent and stable.
EKG strip shows sinus tachycardia at this time.
I'll take a quick look at your skin - no rashes, bruising, or other signs of trauma.
Skin is warm, dry, and within normal limits.
Pupil assessment is limited due to altered mental status.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - mental status
dialogue.utterance: I'll now assess your mental status. What's your name?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - mental status
dialogue.utterance: Don't remember.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - mental status
dialogue.utterance: Can you tell me the day of the week?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - mental status
dialogue.utterance: Not sure.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - mental status
dialogue.utterance: His GCS is 11 - Eye 3, Verbal 3, Motor 5 - consistent with the narrative.
We observed minor twitching in the head and shoulders - likely documented in the initial report.
Are you experiencing any pain or discomfort right now?
Not really.
We record no pain reported - assigned a pain scale of 0 in our field record.
Do you have any known allergies that might concern us?
No.
Are you on any medications or taking supplements currently?
No, not that I can recall.
When was the last time you ate or had something to drink?
Not sure.
Have you had any major medical history or surgeries we should know about?
I don't remember.

dialogue.micro_intent: take procedures - cv ecg 4 lead monitoring
dialogue.utterance: We will apply this heart monitor to your chest - it's for your safety evaluation.

dialogue.micro_intent: take procedures - cv ecg 4 lead monitoring
dialogue.utterance: The monitor is not painful and will remain while we monitor your cardiac activity.
I'm going to check your blood glucose now using this portable device.
The reading is at zero - we're unable to detect any measurement at this time.
Given his status, we're using the opioid overdose/poisoning protocol, 7-1.

dialogue.micro_intent: take procedures - iv start - extremity vein
dialogue.utterance: I need to place an IV line in your left forearm - it will help us if medications are needed.

dialogue.micro_intent: take procedures - iv start - extremity vein
dialogue.utterance: You may feel a small pinch when the needle goes in - we'll secure the site right away.
Your latest pulse is 97 - marked improvement from the first reading.
Breathing is now 14 per minute - steady and regular.
Updated blood pressure is 139 over 76.
Still no reading from the device - reading remains at 0.
Oxygen saturation is holding steady at 97 percent.
The EKG still shows sinus tachycardia - we've seen no change at this time.
Given the field impression, we're taking him to VCU Medical Center - the closest appropriate hospital.
Take him to the closest hospital.
I'll assist you onto the stretcher and help you lie down for transport.
He walked into the ambulance - we'll place him on the stretcher and secure him there.
Based on the protocol, I suspect a possible opioid episode and will document accordingly.
We've completed 4-lead ECG monitoring and placed an IV in the left forearm.
GCS is 11 - A&O*1, meaning he is inconsistently responding to verbal stimuli.
We took him to VCU Health Systems and transferred him to Room 15.
We observed some head and shoulder twitching - supportable in the narrative.
We rechecked vitals after interventions and noted positive trend in numbers.
Skin was examined and in normal appearance with no trauma.
He experienced limited communication likely due to his altered mental status.
He denied drug use and drinking, but we note suspicion due to behavioral indicators.
EKG and IV were part of screening due to his mental status.
We found his breathing clear with no signs of abnormality.
At times, he moaned, but without resistance or aggression.
Transport was incident-free from the scene to the hospital.
Patient is male, 53 years old, and weighs 86 kilograms.
Handcuffs were removed per instructions from R-PD before transport.
His mental state fluctuated throughout: calm periods and twitching.
We reported a complete handoff to both the RN and MD at the facility.
He showed no significant agitation or resistance.
No history of medications or surgical conditions were reported.
No neurological symptoms detected in our assessment.
Midline trachea and no neck vein abnormalities were observed.
No visible injuries or trauma were found during the physical.
He sat calmly and walked into the ambulance without issues.
No significant delay occurred prior to transport.
Equal breath sounds in both lungs, no signs of distress.
The opioid protocol was followed throughout assessment and reassessment.
Pulse strength was normal and easily palpated.
We saw post IV placement overall stabilization in vitals.
All interventions and vitals were properly timed and recorded.
Oxygen saturation remained above 95% for monitoring.
No cyanosis or color change was noted in extremities.
Skin turgor and tactile temperature were normal and documented.
No special handling or secondary risks were observed.
A pulse oximeter was in use for continuous monitoring.
He was placed on the stretcher with no family members present.
Left forearm IV site is intact and secure for transport.
IV inserted without complications and remains secure.
Blood pressure and heart rate improved post IV insertion.
Final vitals captured at handoff - BP 139/76, pulse 97.
All emergency responses were documented and followed protocol.
